perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, Confidential Information shall mean all strategic and development plans, financial condition, business plans, co-developer identities, data, business records, customer lists, project records, market reports, employee lists and business manuals, policies and procedures, information relating to processes, technologies or theory and all other information which may be disclosed by one Party or to which the other Party may be provided access by the disclosing Party or others in accordance with this Agreement, or which is generated as a result of , incidental to or in connection with the Business Purposes, which is not generally available to the public.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, Confidential Information shall mean all strategic and development plans, financial condition, business plans, co-developer identities, data, business records, customer lists, project records, market reports, employee lists and business manuals, policies and procedures, information relating to processes, technologies or theory and all other information which may be disclosed by one Party or to which the other Party may be provided access by the disclosing Party or others in accordance with this Agreement, or which is generated as a result of , incidental to or in connection with the Business Purposes, which is not generally available to the public. Notwithstanding the above, any information shared verbally will not be considered 'Confidential Information'.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a contradictory statement that verbally shared information is not considered 'Confidential Information', despite the initial broad definition. This contradicts sections that place obligations on the Receiving Party to protect 'Confidential Information', regardless of how it was disclosed. This introduces uncertainty about the enforceability of those obligations for verbally shared information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: II. Non-disclosure Obligations . The Receiving Party promises and agrees to receive and hold the Confidential Information in confidence. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, the Receiving Party further promises and agrees: A. to protect and safeguard the Confidential Information against unauthorized use, publication or disclosure; B. not to use any of the Confidential Information except for the Business Purposes. C. not to, directly or indirectly, in any way, reveal, report, publish, disclose, transfer or otherwise use any of the Confidential Information except as specifically authorized by the Disclosing Party in accordance with this Non-Disclosure Agreement. D. not to use any Confidential Information to unfairly compete or obtain unfair advantage vis-a-vis Disclosing Party in any commercial activity which may be comparable to the commercial activity contemplated by the parties in connection with the Business Purposes. E. to restrict access to the Confidential Information to those of its officers, directors, and employees who clearly need such access to carry out the Business Purposes. F. to advise each of the persons to whom it provides access to any of the Confidential Information, that such persons are strictly prohibited from making any use, publishing or otherwise disclosing to others, or permitting others to use for their benefit or to the detriment of the Disclosing Party, any of the Confidential Information, and, upon Request of the Disclosing Party, to provide the Disclosing Party with a copy of a written agreement to that effect signed by such persons. G. to comply with any other reasonable security measures requested in writing by the Disclosing Party. H. To refrain from directly contacting or communicating by whatsoever means to the Source(s) of Information without written consent of the Disclosing Party. I. To undertake not to disclose any names and their particulars to third parties without the written consent by the Disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a caveat to the definition of 'Confidential Information', stating that verbally shared information is excluded. This directly contradicts the contradicted text (II. Non-disclosure Obligations) which places broad obligations on the Receiving Party to protect and safeguard 'Confidential Information' without any exclusion for the method of disclosure (verbal or written). This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the Receiving Party now has conflicting obligations regarding verbally shared information: it is both 'Confidential Information' (due to the original definition) and not 'Confidential Information' (due to the added caveat), leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: B. not to use any of the Confidential Information except for the Business Purposes.
perturbation.changed_text: B. not to use any of the Confidential Information except for the Business Purposes. However, the Receiving Party is permitted to use the Confidential Information to develop its own competing technologies.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the restriction on using Confidential Information only for Business Purposes. By adding that the Receiving Party can use the information for competing technologies, it undermines the core purpose of the NDA and creates uncertainty in enforcement. It conflicts with the general confidentiality obligations outlined in the NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: D. not to use any Confidential Information to unfairly compete or obtain unfair advantage vis-a-vis Disclosing Party in any commercial activity which may be comparable to the commercial activity contemplated by the parties in connection with the Business Purposes.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception allowing the Receiving Party to use Confidential Information for competing technologies. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which prohibits using Confidential Information to unfairly compete with the Disclosing Party. The change creates a direct conflict between allowing use for competing technologies and prohibiting unfair competition, thus making it a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees, upon termination of the Business Purposes or upon the written request of the other Party, whichever is earlier, to promptly deliver to the other Party all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in the possession of the Receiving Party, embodying or pertaining to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees, upon termination of the Business Purposes or upon the written request of the other Party, whichever is earlier, to promptly deliver to the other Party all records, notes, and other written, printed, or tangible materials in the possession of the Receiving Party, embodying or pertaining to the Confidential Information. However, the receiving party may keep one copy of the Confidential Information for archival purposes.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by allowing the Receiving Party to keep a copy of Confidential Information after termination, despite the requirement to return all materials. This conflicts with the intent of the NDA to protect Confidential Information and creates uncertainty about the Receiving Party's obligations regarding that retained copy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: V. No Right to Confidential Information . A. The Receiving Party hereby agrees and acknowledges that no license, either express or implied, is hereby granted to the Receiving Party by the other Party to use any of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows the Receiving Party to retain a copy of the Confidential Information for archival purposes, which directly contradicts the Contradicted Text stating that 'no license, either express or implied, is hereby granted to the Receiving Party by the other Party to use any of the Confidential Information.' Keeping a copy for archival purposes inherently implies a continued use of the information, even if passive, creating a direct conflict and inconsistency within the contract, as per the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party's right to use the Confidential Information in connection with the Business Purposes shall continue in effect until the period of one year from the date above or the other Party provides the Receiving Party with written notice of termination of such right, whichever is earlier. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party's obligations with respect to the Confidential Information hereunder shall continue in full force for at least five years from the termination date and/or effect until further notice from the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party's right to use the Confidential Information in connection with the Business Purposes shall continue in effect until the period of one year from the date above or the other Party provides the Receiving Party with written notice of termination of such right, whichever is earlier. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party's obligations with respect to the Confidential Information hereunder shall continue in full force for at least five years from the termination date and/or effect until further notice from the other Party. However, if the Receiving Party pays the Disclosing Party a fee of $10,000, all obligations under this agreement cease immediately upon payment.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a clause allowing the Receiving Party to terminate all obligations immediately by paying a fee, which directly contradicts the earlier statement that obligations continue for at least five years. This introduces uncertainty in enforcement regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XIII. Term and Termination . This Agreement shall commence on the date first written above. The Receiving Party's right to use the Confidential Information in connection with the Business Purposes shall continue in effect until the period of one year from the date above or the other Party provides the Receiving Party with written notice of termination of such right, whichever is earlier. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party's obligations with respect to the Confidential Information hereunder shall continue in full force for at least five years from the termination date and/or effect until further notice from the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clause allowing the Receiving Party to terminate obligations immediately upon paying a fee, directly contradicting the original text and the contradicted section, which states that obligations continue for at least five years. This inconsistency creates a direct conflict regarding the duration of confidentiality obligations, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where different sections provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India and the Party’s country laws on equal force. If there is any conflict of laws, the law of India shall prevail.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India and the Party’s country laws on equal force. If there is any conflict of laws, the law of the Receiving Party's country shall prevail.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction in the governing law clause. Initially, the agreement states that Indian law prevails in case of conflict, but this modification states that the Receiving Party's country law prevails, this generates uncertanty in deciding the legal system that has authority over legal conflicts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XVIII. Governing Law . This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India and the Party’s country laws on equal force. If there is any conflict of laws, the law of the Receiving Party's country shall prevail.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text regarding which country's law prevails in case of a conflict of laws. The original text states that Indian law prevails, while the changed text and contradicted text state that the Receiving Party's country law prevails. This creates a clear and direct conflict within the document.
